actions: Read external/README.md (full 283 lines) — benchmark methodology section at lines 31-48: SWE-bench Verified + mini-swe-agent, Sonnet 4.0 baseline 66.0% → +XCE 73.4% (+7.4pp), MiniMax M2.5 75.8% → 78.2% (+2.4pp), chart at line 34 (assets/xce-benchmarks.png), token reduction claim at line 188 '~20% token reduction when the agent uses XCE proactively'

Read vitest.stress.config.ts (35 lines) — STRESS_TIMEOUT_MS=120000, fileParallelism=false, includes mcp_server/stress_test/** only, excludes mcp_server/tests/**, code_graph/tests/**, skill_advisor/tests/**

Read mcp_server/tests/eval-metrics.vitest.ts (625 lines) — 12 eval metrics tested: computeMRR, computeNDCG, computeRecall, computeHitRate, computePrecision, computeF1, computeMAP, computeInversionRate, computeConstitutionalSurfacingRate, computeImportanceWeightedRecall, computeColdStartDetectionRate, computeIntentWeightedNDCG; all pure-computation from lib/eval/eval-metrics.ts, computeAllMetrics() convenience wrapper

Read mcp_server/tests/smart-router-measurement.vitest.ts (184 lines) — existing static measurement harness: labeled CorpusRow[] with skill_top_1 ground truth, runMeasurement() with buildBrief injection, advisor accuracy computation, markdown report with caveat 'It does not measure actual AI tool reads'

Read scripts/observability/smart-router-measurement.ts (841 lines) — measurement engine: CorpusRow interface (lines 23-27), MeasurementOptions (lines 84-95), runMeasurement() loop (lines 641-689: for each row → buildBrief → predict route → compute accuracy → record telemetry → output JSONL + markdown). predictSmartRouterRoute() (lines 516-573) parses skill SKILL.md for SMART ROUTING section
Read mcp_server/scripts/README.md (116 lines) — confirms scripts are compatibility wrappers delegating to canonical workspace scripts/

Glob mcp_server/lib/eval/ — 15-file eval framework: eval-metrics.ts, ground-truth-generator.ts, ablation-framework.ts, warm-start-variant-runner.ts, eval-db.ts, eval-logger.ts, bm25-baseline.ts, reporting-dashboard.ts, k-value-analysis.ts, ground-truth-feedback.ts, edge-density.ts, shadow-scoring.ts, ground-truth-data.ts, eval-quality-proxy.ts, memory-state-baseline.ts
Read iteration-006.md — prior iter focus RQ6, next-focus RQ7

label: XCE benchmark methodology — SWE-bench Verified with mini-swe-agent. Resolve rates: Sonnet 4.0 66.0% baseline → 73.4% with XCE (+7.4pp). Uses Docker sandboxes, closed-source scripts. NOT replicable locally (~50GB infrastructure). Our lighter alternative: 12-20 refactoring tasks on our own repo measuring file-reads-avoided / context-accuracy / answer-completeness.

evidence.quote: All results on SWE-bench Verified using mini-swe-agent
Sonnet 4.0 (baseline) 66.0% | Sonnet 4.0 + XCE 73.4% | Sonnet 4.0 + XCE (cascade hybrid) 76.8% | MiniMax M2.5 (baseline) 75.8% | MiniMax M2.5 + XCE 78.2%
An older-generation model (Sonnet 4.0) with XCE beats raw Sonnet 4.6 and reaches Opus-level performance — at 16x lower cost.
~20% token reduction when the agent uses XCE proactively

verdict: DEFER — SWE-bench too heavy; propose lighter local harness in sub-packet 028

label: Existing eval infrastructure reusable for code_graph adoption harness: (a) 12 pure-computation metrics in lib/eval/eval-metrics.ts — HitRate@1, MRR, Recall, etc. for context-accuracy measurement. (b) Static measurement harness in scripts/observability/smart-router-measurement.ts — CorpusRow → runMeasurement() loop → markdown report pattern. (c) 15-file eval framework in lib/eval/ — ground truth generator, ablation framework, warm-start runner. (d) vitest.stress.config.ts — separate stress test config with fileParallelism=false, 120s timeout for long-running eval.

evidence.quote: import { computeMRR, computeNDCG, computeRecall, computeHitRate, ... computeAllMetrics } from '../lib/eval/eval-metrics' — 12 metrics tested, all pure-computation
CorpusRow interface with id, prompt, skill_top_1 — labeled prompt dataset pattern
runMeasurement() loop: for each row → buildBrief → predict route → compute accuracy → record telemetry — dispatcher pattern template

verdict: REUSE — existing eval-metrics + smart-router-measurement patterns form foundation for adoption harness

label: Proposed eval harness: (1) Task set: 12-20 refactoring tasks on system-spec-kit codebase in labeled-tasks.jsonl. (2) Metrics: M1 file-reads-avoided (grep_min_reads − agent_actual_reads) / grep_min_reads; M2 context-accuracy (HitRate@1 of code_graph results vs ground truth); M3 answer-completeness (file-set Jaccard + diff comparison). Diagnostics D1 token-waste-ratio, D2 first-action-adherence. (3) Protocol: Phase 1 BASELINE (code_graph disabled) → Phase 2 AFTER (code_graph enabled + MUST invoke brief) → paired per-task comparison. (4) Dispatcher: CLI entry point spawning OpenCode subprocess per task, collecting tool-call logs, emitting JSONL + markdown report. Per-task timeout 10 min. Estimated ~350-450 LOC.

evidence.quote: runMeasurement() loop pattern: for each row → call buildBrief → compute correctness → aggregate — foundational dispatcher template
computeHitRate() tests — 'Top result is relevant → Hit Rate = 1.0', 'Top result is not relevant → Hit Rate = 0.0' — reused for M2 context-accuracy
STRESS_TIMEOUT_MS = 120_000; TEARDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS = 1_000 — pattern for long-running eval timeout (10 min/task for our harness)

verdict: DEFER — full harness design specified; implementation deferred to sub-packet 028 (~400 LOC)

label: Verdict DEFER (P2 sub-packet 028-code-graph-adoption-eval). Not ADOPT (research-only packet prohibits implementation; harness depends on RQ6 render change and subprocess dispatch). Not ADAPT (existing smart-router-measurement measures static advisor accuracy on prompts, not agent behavior on real tasks with file-read tracking). Not SKIP (harness is measurement foundation for RQ8 token-reduction claim — without it RQ8 is unfalsifiable). Sub-packet: Level 2, ~400 LOC, depends on RQ6 first-action mandate change, feeds RQ8 measurement protocol.

evidence.quote: REQ-010 | Benchmark methodology proposal | sub-packet-proposals.md includes candidate 'evaluation harness' sub-packet describing how we'd measure file-reads-avoided / context-accuracy on a held-out task set, even if scope-deferred — P2 scope
'It does not measure actual AI tool reads' — existing harness acknowledges this limitation, confirming new harness is needed
~20% token reduction when the agent uses XCE proactively — RQ8's primary claim, which 028 eval harness would measure

verdict: DEFER — P2 sub-packet 028-code-graph-adoption-eval, ~400 LOC, depends on RQ6 render change, feeds RQ8
